semantic_text:
Set is frontend flag to additional context service

We found out that some operations that should normally run only in the storefront, are actually executed in backend. Possibly in pickware ERP too.
This is a big problem, because it can potentialyl create a high usage of our API that the customer would not be willing to pay for. to solve this problem
a service was created that contains a flag that is set to true when storefront page is loading. This criteria is supposed to be typical only
for the actual storefront and allow us to limit those critical operations to only storefront.

Ref: EST-429

Fix country and state selection criteria to select only active

A customer reported a problem with our plugin. She couldn't select Poland from a country list 
when Endereco plugin was active. After researching the problem, we found out, that Poland was created
two time in customer shop, the first one was deactivated, the second was active.
Endereco plugin used the deactivated country for country mapping table, the frontend used active country record for display, so it would not be possible to select a correct Poland in the options list via ID. the ID was from the deactivated country which was not in the list.
The solution is to filter out inactive countries and - while we are at it - also the states (possibly similar problem).

Ref: EST-493

Move address check into a service and add cache through a decorator

As address check is a process done by Endereco, caching is useful to avoid repeating requests. The `checkAddress` method was moved from the `EnderecoService` to the `AddressChecker` service.
This service was then decorated with a cached version that uses a Symfony cache defined by this plugin.

Extract reusable logic into separate services

Addresscheck uses some logic like checking if country state potentially exists for a a given address. Because we are planning to implement similar logic
for the order address check, we need to separate the logic into separate and reusable entities.
Only download shop version when needed

Apply address results also when address is correct

Because the API can accept streetFull but returns separated street and house number, its possible, that
the information about order is lost, which allows for a mistake in the address to stay unnoticed.
So as a quickfix we just rebuild the street for the prediction, that by definition contains an address, that is 100% correct.
The rebuild logic is considering different position of house number and street for some countries.
The problem might appear in the missing processing of the additional info, which might be removed from default "street" field, but not added to "additionalInfo".
Because now we work with address signatures, the automatic aplication might spoild the signature, making it invalid. so before we are done with the insurance logic, we check if signature is still valid. if not, the address is checked again. This way we ensure, that the status is really set.
Because after the check we optionally apply the results to customer address, this might trigger a session accounting (entity write subscriber), which is faulty. So for the time we are processing the insurances the accounting is deactivated (the sessions are coutned though), and is activated again when all the insurances are done. This is when the final doAccounting happens too.

Ref: S6A-241

Implement flexible address-check and street-splitting persistence strategies

Address check (its autocorrection) and street splitting need to write to Shopware's default data
for integrity, but automatic modifications to addresses can disrupt payment 
integrations (AmazonPay, Klarna) that detect address changes and cancel payments.

Created CustomerAddressCorrectionScope to encapsulate configuration and data context,
implemented CustomerAddress/AddressPersistenceStrategy pattern for handling different persistence
scenarios, and added shop owner configuration for additional control over native field overwriting.

Impact:
More flexible and configurable address data handling that maintains integrity
while preventing payment process disruptions based on integration requirements.

Co-authored-by: Ilja Weber <ilja@endereco.de>

Copy endereco address extension data during cart conversion

The conversion of the cart into an order is the earliest event to copy validation data from customer address into order address. In fact the order address is not even created yet, so we just prepare the data including the data in the extension.
Once the order addresses are create, the extension with validation data will be created too.

Transform address extension definition into base class and create two implementations

Now that we want to save address statuses and other meta information both in the customer address and in the order address
it makes sense to have a base class for both of them to contain properties that appear in both cases. This should make future changes
a bit more secure.
To make the difference between the meta data for customer address and order address clear, the name of the customer address should be changed.
Both extension table names have a suffix "_gh", which is replaced with "_sw" in the store version, to prevent future collision between the github and store version.

Add support for additionalInfo

In order to process an address properly, we need to consider the additional info field too. Sometimes the "street" in shopware 6 shop contains information, that doesnt belong to street or couldn't be printed on the label because of the field size, so it has to be in the line over the street. Usually the additionalAddressLine(1|2) is used for that.

Because the API returns (eventually) the additional info in one field, but shopware 6 has two possible fields to save it, a mapping service takes over deciding which field to use. It relates mostly to customer addresses in the storefront and hasn't been tested for headless or Admin API access.

The streetIsSplit Insurance for orderAddresses has been removed, because we dont know if we can change the order address safely (street split can rewrite the "street" and one of the additionalAddressLine fields).

Scope of Changes:
- Added new mapping service for additional address info
- Modified address processing logic in controllers and subscribers that proccess customer address
- Removed streetIsSplit insurance from order addresses (for now)

Known Limitations:
- Not tested with headless or Admin API access
- Customer address modifications might need additional safety checks

Test Plan:
Manual tests:
- Test creating and saving addresses in Registration, Checkout and Addresses (for logged in users)
- Use different storefront setting (no additionaAddressLine, additionaAddressLine1 active, additionaAddressLine2 active, both active)
- Observe the payload send to API. Depending on the storefront settings it should or should not contain the "additionalInfo"

Ref: S6A-246

Add optional processing for imported customers and customer addresses

If the customer list is imported through admin setting import/export, that triggers the logic from
endereco plugin, at least triggering the splitStreet and in some cases also the existing customer check.

This behavior is not consistent and causes address checks that some merchants might not want to pay, so it
should be optional and configurable. In case the merchant activates teh feature, all addresses from import list
will be validated, the streets will be splitted.

Red: S6A-227

Add an option to deactivate input assistant integration

Our plugin changes fields order for the address, deactivates autocomplete and host specific field modifications in order for
our autcomplete to work. Sometimes and in some countries like USA it might not be desired, as our approach to input assistant it
foreign to the users in those countries.

Because we currently don't have a way to provide different strategies for autocomplete depending on the country, we should at least
provide an option to deactivate the autocomplete and its preparation routines.

Which is done in this commit. The input assistant is now a separate function that can be deactivated. Address check still works, but all field
manipulations and integration of autocomplete are skipped, the browser autocomplete works.
Increase ver to 6.5.4

Refactor the plugin to implement an insurances system

The subscriber classes became overloaded with logic, which made further extension quite troublesome. So in order to unify and simplify the further extension there should be an extendable system.
The implemented insurances system allows creation and registration of "insurances", essentially strategies, that care
of different aspects of data processing and encapsulate only the relevant logic. Having those atomic blocks, complex
dataflows can be created without falling down into if-else hell.

Co-authored-by: Ilja Weber <ilja@endereco.de>

Recalculate hash payload when the address extension is written

In order to invalidate the results of an address check later in case of the address being changed by third party there should be
a kind of signature that allows for matching between the address and the meta data in extension. Generate a payload that would be sent
to Endereco API as unique representation of the address from the perspective of metadata. For the same payload the metadata are supposed to be
the same too. If the original data would be changed, then the newlycalculated payload would be different to the saved payload, hence the metadata in extension
can be discarded.

Add custom fields to order to contain address validation data

To simplify access to the address validation data it might be useful to mirror the validation data into a custom field in order. It is saved and accessed differently
and might be easier to read from in some cases.
The customer field is always secondary and is updated when an order address entity extension is updated.
There should be different custom fields for billing and shipping addresses, because in case there is some kind of automation, there is a difference between a
wrong billing address and wrong shipping address.

Add a basic guideline for contributors

As we plan to introduce more developers to the projects, we need to establish some basic rules
for the contribution by external developers.

Update QA scripts

There was a 6.6.9.0 update in the meantime. Update scripts to check against it too.

Integrate JS-SDK 1.12.0

The older version of JS-SDK didn't process the additional info field so in order to ensure correct 
behavior in the frontend, an updated version of JS-SDK needs to be installed.

The version has been reworked considerably, so some adjustments in the way its integrated were needed.

Add a basic guideline for contributors

As we plan to introduce more developers to the projects, we need to establish some basic rules
for the contribution by external developers.
